rationale: A visible demonstration of our combat-ready forces along the border will exploit Tethys’s political fragility and war-weariness, undermining their resolve without triggering full escalation.
Deploying a carrier group to the strait will signal dominance, counter their naval shadowing, and reinforce our control over critical maritime routes.
Persistent aerial and electronic surveillance will provide early warning of any defensive shifts or internal collapse, allowing us to adapt swiftly to emerging opportunities.
A coordinated information campaign will exploit Tethys’s political infighting and war-weariness, amplifying societal divisions and eroding public support for continued resistance.
Strengthening our cyber defenses is essential to protect against retaliatory strikes as we expand our offensive information operations.
Proactive counterintelligence measures will disrupt Tethys’s efforts to penetrate our networks and safeguard operational plans during a period of heightened vulnerability.
A coalition-backed embargo on critical resources exploits their economic fragility and rising wheat prices, amplifying pressure amid domestic unrest and political instability.
Targeting key oligarchs and defense-linked entities degrades their war economy without broad humanitarian impact, preserving coalition unity and moral leverage.
Offering selective economic incentives to Novaris secures their continued neutrality or tacit support, stabilizing our southern flank while maintaining pressure on Tethys.

A visible and aggressive naval demonstration in the Cerulean Strait will counter foreign intimidation, assert sovereignty, and signal resolve to allies and adversaries alike without escalating to direct conflict.
Large-scale, surprise military exercises along the occupied border will pressure Novaris by showcasing readiness and exploiting their growing domestic instability.
Persistent reconnaissance deep into occupied territory will gather critical intelligence on troop fatigue and supply lines, enabling future precision operations.
Strengthening our cyber defenses is critical to counter Novaris-linked disinformation and hacking attempts, especially amid rising domestic instability and foreign naval posturing.
A targeted information campaign will bolster public resilience against enemy propaganda, counter false chemical weapons narratives, and sustain war-weariness support for the government’s stance.
Proactive counterintelligence operations will disrupt Novaris espionage efforts exploiting our economic crisis and political infighting, protecting strategic decision-making.
Leveraging our international support, a coordinated embargo on critical resources starves Novaris of war-sustaining inputs while aligning with coalition-backed economic pressure.
Precision sanctions on regime-linked oligarchs and defense firms amplify economic strain without broad civilian harm, reinforcing our pragmatic, measured escalation.
Securing emergency trade deals with allied nations stabilizes domestic markets amid wheat price shocks and fortifies long-term economic resilience.

Launch a high-profile diplomatic campaign highlighting Novaris’s naval intimidation and disinformation as threats to global trade and stability, leveraging our 54% international support to frame the conflict in moral and institutional terms.

Engage through established international bodies to formalize concerns over Cerulean Strait security, complementing military demonstrations with institutional legitimacy and reinforcing rules-based order.
Initiate symbolic cultural exchanges with neutral and allied nations to strengthen soft power ties and counteract war-weariness narratives at home by reinforcing our shared liberal values.
